zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Pitres於1085年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 白 希雷斯特時，她的父親Walter FitzRoger20歲，母親Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluun16歲。她於大約1097年與Richard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychan在英國格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1127年之後在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud de Pitres于1085年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 白 希雷斯特时，她的父亲Walter FitzRoger20岁，母亲Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluun16岁。她于大约1097年与Richard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychan在英国格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1127年之后在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Pitres
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Raoul, Emile, Armand, Monique, Alcid, Amedee, Anatole, Andrus, Aubert, Camille, Cecile, Clovis.

French: variant of Pistre, itself a variant of Pestre, an occupational name for a baker or pastry cook, from an agent derivative of Old French pester ‘to knead’. Compare Peat , Peet , Peete , Peets , Pete , Peters , and Petre .
French: habitational name from Pîtres in Eure.

variants.name: Peat
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English and Older Scots personal name Peet, a pet form of Peter .
English and Scottish: nickname from Middle English pete ‘peat’, perhaps for someone who cut or sold peat.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Pitres was born in 1085, in Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Walter FitzRoger, was 20 and her mother, Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluun, was 16. She married Richard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychan about 1097, in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died after 1127, in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Pitres est née en 1085, à Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Walter FitzRoger, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluun, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé Richard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychan environ 1097, à Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1127, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud de Pitres 1085 in Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter FitzRoger 20 und ihre Mutter Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluun 16. Sie hat um 1097 in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England Richard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1127 in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Pitres è nata il nel 1085 nel Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, sua padre, Walter FitzRoger, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluun, aveva 16 anni. Lei sposato Richard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychan circa 1097, nel Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1127, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud de Pitresが1085年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン ビー シアーエンセスターで生まれた時，父Walter FitzRogerは20歳，母Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluunは16歳であった。約1097年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリンプスフィールドでRichard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1127年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Pitres(이)가 1085년 영국 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter FitzRoger(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluun(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1097년 영국 그루스스터실르 브림프스필드에서 Richard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1127년 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Pitres nasceu em 1085, em Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walter FitzRoger, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluun, tinha 16 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychan aproximadamente 1097, em Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1127, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Pitres родилась в 1085 г. в местности фрамптон би сиренсестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Walter FitzRoger было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluun, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychan около 1097 г. в местности бримпсфиелд, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1127 г. в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud de Pitres nació en 1085, en Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter FitzRoger, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Emmeline Berthe de Pitres de Baluun, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Richard FitzPons Lord of Cantref Bychan aproximadamente 1097, en Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió después de 1127, en su pueblo.
